title: Shared Lexical Task Representations Explain Behavioral Variability In LLMs
summary: One of the most common complaints about large language models (LLMs) is their prompt sensitivity -- that is, the fact that their ability to perform a task or provide a correct answer to a question can depend unpredictably on the way the question is posed. We investigate this variation by comparing two very different but commonly-used styles of prompting: instruction-based prompts, which describe the task in natural language, and example-based prompts, which provide in-context few-shot demonstration pairs to illustrate the task. We find that, despite large variation in performance as a funct...

metadata.comment: Finds shared lexical task heads across prompt styles and links prompt sensitivity to activation strength of internal task representations.

title: SOC-ICNN: From Polyhedral to Conic Geometry for Learning Convex Surrogate Functions
summary: Classical ReLU-based Input Convex Neural Networks (ICNNs) are equivalent to the optimal value functions of Linear Programming (LP). This intrinsic structural equivalence restricts their representational capacity to piecewise-linear polyhedral functions. To overcome this representational bottleneck, we propose the SOC-ICNN, an architecture that generalizes the underlying optimization class from LP to Second-Order Cone Programming (SOCP). By explicitly injecting positive semi-definite curvature and Euclidean norm-based conic primitives, our formulation introduces native smooth curvature into...

metadata.comment: Extends ICNNs from LP-equivalent polyhedral representations to SOCP-based conic geometry with provably richer convex function classes.

title: Near-Optimal Regret for the Safe Learning-based Control of the Constrained Linear Quadratic Regulator
summary: We study the problem of adaptive control of the stochastic linear quadratic regulator (LQR) with constraints that must be satisfied at every time step. Prior work on the multidimensional problem has shown $\tilde{O}(T^{2/3})$ regret and satisfaction of robust constraints, leaving open the question of whether $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret can be attained in the constrained LQR setting. We contribute to this problem by showing $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret and satisfaction of chance constraints. This type of constraints allow us to handle unbounded noise and also enable analytical techniques no...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Achieves near-optimal regret for constrained adaptive LQR with always-safe chance constraints via optimistic control plus safety scaling.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: HGQ-LUT: Fast LUT-Aware Training and Efficient Architectures for DNN Inference
summary: Lookup-table (LUT) based neural networks can deliver ultra-low latency and excellent hardware efficiency on FPGAs by mapping arithmetic operations directly onto the logic primitives. However, state-of-the-art LUT-aware training (LAT) approaches remain difficult to use in practice: they are often orders of magnitude slower to train than conventional networks, require non-trivial manual tuning for hardware efficiency, and lack an end-to-end workflow. This work presents HGQ-LUT, integrated in https://github.com/calad0i/HGQ2, a new LAT approach that achieves state-of-the-art hardware efficiency...

metadata.comment: Makes LUT-aware training practical by redesigning LUT layers for standard tensor ops and adding automated accuracy-resource co-optimization.

title: Relaxation-Informed Training of Neural Network Surrogate Models
summary: ReLU neural networks trained as surrogate models can be embedded exactly in mixed-integer linear programs (MILPs), enabling global optimization over the learned function. The tractability of the resulting MILP depends on structural properties of the network, i.e., the number of binary variables in associated formulations and the tightness of the continuous LP relaxation. These properties are determined during training, yet standard training objectives (prediction loss with classical weight regularization) offer no mechanism to directly control them. This work studies training regularizers t...

metadata.comment: Regularizes neural-network training using MILP big-M bounds, unstable-neuron counts, and LP relaxation gaps to improve downstream optimization tractability.

title: Universal Transformers Need Memory: Depth-State Trade-offs in Adaptive Recursive Reasoning
summary: The explicit claim that adaptive universal transformers need internal scratchpad memory for recursive reasoning points toward a potentially important design direction.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Demonstrates memory tokens are necessary for single-block Universal Transformers with ACT, and identifies an ACT initialization failure mode.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
metadata.spotlight_comment: The explicit claim that adaptive universal transformers need internal scratchpad memory for recursive reasoning points toward a potentially important design direction.

title: Kernel Contracts: A Specification Language for ML Kernel Correctness Across Heterogeneous Silicon
summary: The contract-based formalization of ML kernel correctness across silicon looks like a new systems direction rather than routine optimization work.

metadata.comment: Defines formal kernel contracts for correctness across heterogeneous ML hardware stacks, targeting reliable large-scale training and inference behavior.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The contract-based formalization of ML kernel correctness across silicon looks like a new systems direction rather than routine optimization work.

title: Hidden Failure Modes of Gradient Modification under Adam in Continual Learning, and Adaptive Decoupled Moment Routing as a Repair
summary: Broadly important today: Explains why gradient modification methods fail under Adam through second-moment dynamics and proposes decoupled moment routing as a fix.

metadata.comment: Explains why gradient modification methods fail under Adam through second-moment dynamics and proposes decoupled moment routing as a fix.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Explains why gradient modification methods fail under Adam through second-moment dynamics and proposes decoupled moment routing as a fix.

title: Dissociating Decodability and Causal Use in Bracket-Sequence Transformers
summary: Broadly important today: Shows a clean decodability-versus-causal-use gap in Dyck transformers by intervening on residual subspaces and stack-like attention.

metadata.comment: Shows a clean decodability-versus-causal-use gap in Dyck transformers by intervening on residual subspaces and stack-like attention.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Shows a clean decodability-versus-causal-use gap in Dyck transformers by intervening on residual subspaces and stack-like attention.

title: How LLMs Detect and Correct Their Own Errors: The Role of Internal Confidence Signals
summary: Mechanistic interpretability result on self-correction in frontier LLMs is likely of broad day-level interest.

metadata.comment: Identifies a cached internal confidence signal that causally supports LLM error detection and self-correction beyond token probabilities.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Mechanistic interpretability result on self-correction in frontier LLMs is likely of broad day-level interest.

title: SpikingBrain2.0: Brain-Inspired Foundation Models for Efficient Long-Context and Cross-Platform Inference
summary: Large long-context foundation-model architecture with substantial efficiency claims and broad interest beyond the specialized list.

metadata.comment: Introduces dual-space sparse attention plus quantized spiking/hybrid inference for long-context efficiency at foundation-model scale.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Large long-context foundation-model architecture with substantial efficiency claims and broad interest beyond the specialized list.

title: Contexts are Never Long Enough: Structured Reasoning for Scalable Question Answering over Long Document Sets
summary: Long-context QA over document sets is broadly timely, and the structured-state approach is likely to draw attention beyond the niche memory-systems audience.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Uses persistent structured state and reconciliation over long document collections, offering a substantive memory organization mechanism beyond context stuffing.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
metadata.spotlight_comment: Long-context QA over document sets is broadly timely, and the structured-state approach is likely to draw attention beyond the niche memory-systems audience.

title: Agentic World Modeling: Foundations, Capabilities, Laws, and Beyond
summary: Broad cross-community synthesis of agentic world modeling likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed as a timely framing paper.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Provides a unifying taxonomy and evaluation framework for agentic world models across physical, digital, social, and scientific regimes.
